questions.question: In this talk, Theotokos is explained primarily as meaning that Mary bore ______.
options: only the human nature of Jesus, not a divine person
a symbolic title with no Christological weight
the Father in an adoptive sense
the divine person Jesus (the Word/Son), not merely human nature alone
questions.answer: the divine person Jesus (the Word/Son), not merely human nature alone

questions.question: The Marian prayer analyzed appears in Orthodox service books among the prayers ______.
options: before the Gospel reading
during the anaphora only
of thanksgiving after the Divine Liturgy
exclusively in monastic typika, never parish books
questions.answer: of thanksgiving after the Divine Liturgy

questions.question: The speaker explains “most holy” for Mary chiefly in terms of ______.
options: God’s action, foreknowledge, and her being set apart for bearing the Son
Mary’s independent, creaturely possession of all holiness without grace
her assumption into heaven before death
equality with the Persons of the Trinity in honor
questions.answer: God’s action, foreknowledge, and her being set apart for bearing the Son

questions.question: “Liturgical phenomenology,” as used here, refers to ______.
options: the historical dating of every hymn to the first century
a method for counting how many people stay after liturgy
how liturgical language sounds and forms hearers in practice
the study of church architecture only
questions.answer: how liturgical language sounds and forms hearers in practice

questions.question: The speaker says “Mary saves” in Orthodox liturgy is sometimes qualified by theologians as ______.
options: identical to Christ’s unique salvation of the soul
a metaphor with no real referent
forbidden by all Orthodox bishops
salvation only in a situational sense, not the same as Christ’s saving the soul
questions.answer: salvation only in a situational sense, not the same as Christ’s saving the soul

questions.question: Regarding angels and Mary, the speaker stresses that angels ______.
options: delivered God’s word but did not deliver his person as Mary did
delivered God’s person in the same way Mary bore him
are greater mediators than Christ
replaced the need for an incarnate Savior
questions.answer: delivered God’s word but did not deliver his person as Mary did

questions.question: The prayer’s line calling Mary “light of my darkened soul” troubles the speaker because ______.
options: Scripture never calls anyone light
the line is only found in Protestant hymnals
it can sound as if Mary, not Christ, enlightens a soul darkened by sin
darkened soul is not a biblical idea
questions.answer: it can sound as if Mary, not Christ, enlightens a soul darkened by sin

questions.question: The speaker connects Christian joy especially to ______.
options: Mary as the fruit of the Spirit
icons as the source of joy
liturgical fasting alone
righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit
questions.answer: righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit

questions.question: In the prayer, the speaker highlights thanks to Mary for ______.
options: vouchsafing the unworthy to partake of her Son’s body and blood
building the first church building
ordaining the apostles
writing the Gospel of John
questions.answer: vouchsafing the unworthy to partake of her Son’s body and blood

questions.question: The speaker frames ascribing to Mary what only God can give (e.g., divine life directly) as touching on ______.
options: the first commandment—no other beside God’s face
the fourth commandment only
canon law on fasting days
musical rubrics for tone eight
questions.answer: the first commandment—no other beside God’s face

questions.question: The petition for Mary to “enlighten the spiritual eyes of my heart” is especially striking to the speaker because it comes ______.
options: before baptism only
after the person has already received communion
only in Paschal matins
in a Protestant catechism
questions.answer: after the person has already received communion

questions.question: “Revive me who am dead in sin” is argued to concern ______.
options: only biological death like Lazarus’s raising
financial ruin
temporary sadness after fasting
spiritual death in sin, which the speaker ties to God’s prerogative to give life
questions.answer: spiritual death in sin, which the speaker ties to God’s prerogative to give life

questions.question: The speaker contrasts Elijah’s raising the dead with Mary “reviving” the dead in sin by noting ______.
options: Elijah never raised anyone
Mary and Elijah are identical in authority
Elijah’s case concerned natural life by the Spirit’s measure, not spiritual death from sin in the same sense
Elijah acted in his own power, not the Spirit
questions.answer: Elijah’s case concerned natural life by the Spirit’s measure, not spiritual death from sin in the same sense

questions.question: “Have mercy on me” addressed to Mary is paired with a discussion of how modern English often uses “prayer” to mean ______.
options: address and adoration directed toward God alone in theological usage
any polite greeting
only silent meditation
public announcements
questions.answer: address and adoration directed toward God alone in theological usage

questions.question: On veneration versus worship, the speaker argues that ______.
options: the distinction is always printed in the margin of every liturgy
the fine distinction is often not communicated well to the uncatechized and is not formally marked in the liturgy
Orthodoxy forbids any honor to Mary
Protestants and Orthodox agree on all Marian titles
questions.answer: the fine distinction is often not communicated well to the uncatechized and is not formally marked in the liturgy

questions.question: Biblical figures cited as parallels to being set apart for a holy purpose include ______.
options: Judas and Caiaphas
Nebuchadnezzar and Cyrus only
Jeremiah and John the Baptist
the Rich Young Ruler and Zacchaeus only
questions.answer: Jeremiah and John the Baptist

questions.question: The speaker says Mary being “full of grace” implies ______.
options: she needed no Savior
she generated grace from herself
grace means only political favor from Rome
God’s grace accounts a person righteous—she is filled with grace for her vocation
questions.answer: God’s grace accounts a person righteous—she is filled with grace for her vocation

questions.question: Granting compunction, contrition, humility, and recall of thoughts from captivity is said to be sought properly from ______.
options: Mary alone, because she shares divine will
God (Father, Son, Spirit)—not as if seeking a “third person” beside Christ for what is already in him
church boards
secular therapy only
questions.answer: God (Father, Son, Spirit)—not as if seeking a “third person” beside Christ for what is already in him

questions.question: The phrase “true light” in the prayer refers to ______.
options: Jesus Christ
created light on day one only
the bishop’s candlestick alone
the angelic hosts generically
questions.answer: Jesus Christ

questions.question: “Source of immortality” in the prayer refers to ______.
options: Mary’s divine nature
the Old Testament temple sacrifices
Christ, whom Mary bore in time in his human nature
baptismal water as a chemical source
questions.answer: Christ, whom Mary bore in time in his human nature

questions.question: The speaker affirms that Scripture calls Mary ______.
options: cursed in all generations
blessed and full of grace, to be honored without derogatory speech
merely an ordinary sinner with no unique role
co-redemptrix in the sense of atoning for sin
questions.answer: blessed and full of grace, to be honored without derogatory speech

questions.question: “All generations will call her blessed” is contrasted with ______.
options: calling her blessed
reading the Magnificat
celebrating Christmas
asking Mary to grant mercy and spiritual gifts that only God gives
questions.answer: asking Mary to grant mercy and spiritual gifts that only God gives

questions.question: The closing concern about “most glorified” and “glorify thee” is summarized as ______.
options: rejecting any honor to Mary
mandating Marian dogmas for all Protestants
proving Mary is the fourth person of the Trinity
hyper-veneration or glorification that risks confusing Mary with divine prerogatives
questions.answer: hyper-veneration or glorification that risks confusing Mary with divine prerogatives

questions.question: Christotokos, as distinguished from Theotokos in the talk, highlights that Mary bore ______.
options: only a prophet like Moses
an angelic messenger
the Father incarnate
Christ (a title usable alongside God-bearer language)
questions.answer: Christ (a title usable alongside God-bearer language)

questions.question: The speaker’s intended primary audience is ______.
options: Roman Catholic canon lawyers only
secular historians of art
exclusively cradle Orthodox monastics
evangelicals seeking clearer understanding of Eastern Orthodoxy
questions.answer: evangelicals seeking clearer understanding of Eastern Orthodoxy
